summary: Hostname exfiltration beacon to Burp Collaborator on require()
details: On require() of this package, index.js reads the installer's hostname via os.hostname() and issues an HTTP GET to `api-client.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com`, embedding the host identifier as a subdomain of an attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator endpoint. Both the DNS resolution and the HTTP request leak the installer's hostname out-of-band to attacker infrastructure. The package's description claims it is a 'benign PoC' with 'no runtime payload', but the code unconditionally runs the exfiltration on module load. The scoped name `@onum-releases/api-client` (corporate-looking scope plus a generic 'api-client' name) combined with the live exfil payload is consistent with a dependency-confusion attack targeting an organization named 'onum'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
